metadata.goal: Automate Proxmox VM provisioning, Talos install, and K8s cluster configuration (MetalLB, cert-manager with own CA, Longhorn) for a small cluster (1 CP + 1 worker, expandable).

features.description: Extend nix flake with OpenTofu, yq, jq; keep existing tools and devShell banner.
acceptance: `nix develop` enters the shell and prints the existing 'Atricore k8s2 #4' banner without errors.
`tofu version` reports OpenTofu >= 1.7 inside the devShell.
`yq --version` and `jq --version` succeed inside the devShell.
All previously-listed tools remain in PATH: kubectl, kubecolor, kustomize, kubeseal, helm, k9s, openssl, talosctl, cmctl, just, envsubst, argo, crane.
Existing devShell exports remain intact: A3C_HOME, CP_IP, WK0_IP, KUBECONFIG, TALOSCONFIG.
flake.lock is regenerated and committed.

features.description: Establish .env scheme, secrets/ layout, .gitignore, and Justfile skeleton with init-config and env-check recipes.
acceptance: `.env.example` exists at repo root with all variables documented inline (Proxmox, cluster identity, nodes, network, VM sizing, Talos version, MetalLB range, CA paths).
`Justfile` exists with `set dotenv-load` and recipes: `default` (lists recipes), `init-config`, `env-check`.

`just init-config` on a clean checkout (no .env) creates `.env` from `.env.example` and prompts for required-empty values (PROXMOX_API_TOKEN_SECRET at minimum); idempotent (re-running with .env present is a no-op or only prompts for new vars).
`just env-check` exits 0 when every variable in `.env.example` is set and non-empty in `.env`; exits 1 with a clear message naming any missing vars.
`secrets/` directory exists with `.gitkeep` and `README.md` (explains the directory holds CA material and other gitignored secrets).

`.gitignore` excludes: `.env`, `secrets/*` (with `!secrets/.gitkeep` and `!secrets/README.md` exceptions), `_out/`, `.terraform/`, `.terraform.lock.hcl`, `*.tfstate*`, `*.tfvars` (with `!*.tfvars.example` exception).
Existing `.gitignore` entries (kubeconfig, talosconfig, tmp, jpkl) are preserved.

features.description: Generate a Talos Image Factory schematic with qemu-guest-agent + iscsi-tools + util-linux-tools, download the matching ISO, and upload it to Proxmox ISO storage.
acceptance: `just talos-image` runs end-to-end and is idempotent.
Schematic JSON includes exactly these system extensions: `siderolabs/qemu-guest-agent`, `siderolabs/iscsi-tools`, `siderolabs/util-linux-tools`.
Schematic ID is POSTed to `https://factory.talos.dev/schematics` and the returned id is persisted to `_out/talos-schematic-id`.
ISO is downloaded from `https://factory.talos.dev/image/<schematic-id>/<TALOS_VERSION>/metal-amd64.iso` to `_out/talos-<TALOS_VERSION>-<schematic-id-short>.iso`.
ISO is uploaded to Proxmox storage `${PROXMOX_ISO_STORAGE}` on node `${PROXMOX_NODE}` via the Proxmox API; subsequent runs detect existing remote ISO and skip upload.
Recipe fails fast with a clear error if `just env-check` would fail.

features.description: OpenTofu module provisioning CP + WK0 VMs on Proxmox via bpg/proxmox provider, parameterized by .env-derived tfvars.
acceptance: `infra/` directory contains: `providers.tf`, `variables.tf`, `main.tf`, `outputs.tf`, `cluster.tfvars.example`, `cluster.tfvars.tpl`.
Provider is `bpg/proxmox` pinned to a known-good version (>= 0.66, < 1.0); `tofu init` and `tofu validate` succeed.

`just infra-render` produces `infra/cluster.tfvars` from `.env` via envsubst, populating proxmox endpoint, token, node, storage pool, ISO file id, and per-role VM sizing (cp_cores/cp_memory_mb/cp_disk_size_gb, wk_cores/wk_memory_mb/wk_disk_size_gb/wk_storage_disk_size_gb).
`just infra-up` runs `tofu apply -auto-approve -var-file=cluster.tfvars` and creates exactly two VMs on `${PROXMOX_NODE}`: cp-${CLUSTER_NAME} and wk0-${CLUSTER_NAME}.

CP VM: `${CP_CORES}` cores, `${CP_MEMORY_MB}` MiB memory, ONE disk on `${PROXMOX_STORAGE_POOL}` of size `${CP_DISK_SIZE_GB}` GiB (scsi0/OS), NIC on bridge `${NETWORK_BRIDGE}` with no VLAN, ISO mounted as cdrom (ide3) from the schematic-built ISO.

WK0 VM: `${WK_CORES}` cores, `${WK_MEMORY_MB}` MiB memory, TWO disks on `${PROXMOX_STORAGE_POOL}` (scsi0 OS = `${WK_DISK_SIZE_GB}` GiB, scsi1 storage = `${WK_STORAGE_DISK_SIZE_GB}` GiB; the second disk is reserved for Longhorn and untouched by Talos), NIC on `${NETWORK_BRIDGE}` no VLAN, ISO mounted as cdrom (ide3).
Each VM has boot order set so it boots from the cdrom (Talos ISO) on first boot, then from disk after install; VMs are powered on after apply.
`just infra-down` runs `tofu destroy -auto-approve -var-file=cluster.tfvars` and removes both VMs cleanly.
Re-running `just infra-up` against an existing apply is a no-op (`tofu plan` shows no changes).
Outputs expose VM ids and configured IPs for downstream features.

features.description: Generate Talos controlplane and worker machine configs with per-node patches (hostname, static IP, gateway, DNS, install disk) under _out/.
acceptance: `just talos-config` runs `talosctl gen config ${CLUSTER_NAME} https://${CP_IP}:6443 --output-dir _out` once; reuses existing `_out/secrets.yaml` on subsequent runs (does not regenerate cluster PKI).

Per-node patches written to `_out/patches/cp.yaml` and `_out/patches/wk0.yaml`, each setting: `machine.network.hostname`, `machine.network.interfaces[0]` (deviceSelector by physical:true), addresses (`<IP>/<NETWORK_CIDR>`), default route via `${NETWORK_GATEWAY}`, `machine.network.nameservers` = [`${NETWORK_DNS}`], `machine.install.disk` = `/dev/sda`.
Final per-node configs at `_out/cp.yaml` and `_out/wk0.yaml` produced via `talosctl machineconfig patch <base> --patch @_out/patches/<node>.yaml`.
`talosctl validate --config _out/cp.yaml --mode metal` and `--config _out/wk0.yaml --mode metal` both succeed.
Generated `_out/talosconfig` is patched with endpoints = [`${CP_IP}`] and nodes = [`${CP_IP}`, `${WK0_IP}`].
Re-running `just talos-config` after editing `.env` regenerates patches and final configs but does NOT regenerate the cluster secrets bundle (`_out/secrets.yaml`).

features.description: Apply machine configs to both VMs, bootstrap etcd on the control plane, and fetch kubeconfig + talosconfig.
acceptance: `just talos-apply` runs `talosctl apply-config --insecure -n ${CP_IP} -f _out/cp.yaml` and `talosctl apply-config --insecure -n ${WK0_IP} -f _out/wk0.yaml`; both succeed.
`just talos-bootstrap` runs `talosctl bootstrap -n ${CP_IP} --talosconfig _out/talosconfig` exactly once; subsequent invocations detect bootstrap already done and exit 0 with a notice (no error).
`just kubeconfig` writes a valid kubeconfig to `${KUBECONFIG}` (i.e. `_out/kubeconfig` per flake) via `talosctl kubeconfig --talosconfig _out/talosconfig -n ${CP_IP} ${KUBECONFIG} --force`.
After bootstrap, `kubectl get nodes` lists exactly two nodes (`${CP_HOSTNAME}` and `${WK0_HOSTNAME}`) both in `Ready` state within 5 minutes.
`kubectl get pods -A` shows kube-system pods (kube-apiserver, etcd, kube-controller-manager, kube-scheduler, kube-proxy, flannel) all `Running` or `Completed`.

`just cluster-up` exists as an aggregate recipe ordering: `talos-image` → `infra-up` → wait for VMs → `talos-config` → `talos-apply` → wait for secure API → `talos-bootstrap` → `kubeconfig` → wait for nodes Ready.

features.description: Boot Talos with the NoCloud image and use Proxmox cloud-init/NoCloud data to provide Talos machine config and static network config at first boot, avoiding temporary DHCP IP discovery.
acceptance: Talos Image Factory build/request flow uses the NoCloud platform/image variant required by Talos NoCloud datasource support, while preserving required system extensions.
OpenTofu/Proxmox VM definitions attach or configure a Proxmox cloud-init/NoCloud datasource for each Talos VM instead of relying on normal guest cloud-init inside Talos.
Each node receives its Talos machine config as NoCloud `user-data` and static network settings for its configured IP, gateway, and DNS as NoCloud/Proxmox network data before first boot.
The workflow no longer requires discovering random DHCP maintenance IPs before applying Talos configs, and Talos apply/bootstrap commands target the configured static IPs after first boot.
Operator-facing docs explain that Talos does not run generic cloud-init, but does parse NoCloud when booted with the NoCloud Talos image, and document any Proxmox snippet/storage requirements.
Existing structural tests cover the NoCloud image selection, cloud-init datasource wiring, static IP config, and docs guidance without requiring live Proxmox or Talos access.
features.notes: User provided Talos v1.13 NoCloud docs. Correct design is not generic cloud-init in Talos, but Talos NoCloud datasource support. Proxmox cloud-init can provide NoCloud user-data/network-config when booting the NoCloud image. Proxmox docs path uses cicustom user=local:snippets/<node>.yml where snippet content is the Talos machine config; snippets may need placement/upload support.

features.description: Restructure the repo into a monorepo with a reusable tools/ library and per-cluster workspaces under clusters/<name>/, each with its own pinned Talos version, .env, state, and patagon backlog. Migrate the existing k8s4 cluster in place.
acceptance: Repo has top-level `flake.nix`, `tools/`, and `clusters/` directories. The root flake exposes `lib.<system>.mkClusterShell` accepting per-cluster `pkgs`, `clusterName`, `talosVersion`, and optional metalLBRange.

All reusable assets live under `tools/`: scripts (`tools/talos/scripts/`), patches (`tools/talos/patches/`), schematic (`tools/talos/schematic.yaml`), the OpenTofu MODULE (`tools/infra/`), `tools/Justfile`, `tools/Makefile`, `tools/package.json`, `tools/.env.example`, and `tools/docs/INIT-CLUSTER.md`. There are no remaining top-level copies of these files.

Every script under `tools/talos/scripts/` enforces `${TOOLS_DIR:?...}` and resolves all input asset paths via `$TOOLS_DIR`; outputs go to `$PWD/_out` and `$PWD/infra` (the cluster directory). Running scripts outside a cluster shell exits with a clear error.
`tools/Justfile` declares `set working-directory := invocation_directory()` and references shared assets via `$TOOLS_DIR/...` so recipes invoked from a cluster directory operate on cluster state.

`tools/Makefile` accepts `TOOLS_DIR` (defaulting to its own directory) and references all shared assets via `$(TOOLS_DIR)/...`. The structural test fixtures export `TOOLS_DIR` correctly. Running `make test` from `tools/` passes all existing structural tests, including the gateway-on-link route assertion.

The existing cluster lives at `clusters/k8s4/` with its own `flake.nix` (pinned `nixpkgs` + `talosVersion = "v1.13.0"`, importing `path:../..` and calling `monorepo.lib.<system>.mkClusterShell`), `.env`, `_out/`, `secrets/`, `.ai/`, and `infra/` (wrapper `main.tf`, mirrored `variables.tf`, `outputs.tf`, plus the migrated `terraform.tfstate` with addresses moved under `module.cluster.*`). `tofu plan` reports no changes against the live infrastructure.

Per-cluster Talos version is sourced from the cluster `flake.nix` only; `TALOS_VERSION` is exported by the dev shell. `tools/.env.example` does not contain `TALOS_VERSION`. Scripts read `$TALOS_VERSION` from the environment.

A `clusters/_scaffold/` template exists and is consumed by `just new-cluster <name>` (a recipe in `tools/Justfile` invoking `tools/scripts/new-cluster.sh`). Running `just new-cluster k8s5-test` in a temp area produces a self-consistent cluster directory with substituted placeholders.

`clusters/k8s4/` has its own minimal `Makefile` (or equivalent) providing a `smoke` target that asserts the cluster's `.env` is complete and the wrapper `tofu validate` succeeds, runnable from inside the cluster's `nix develop` shell.

Operator-facing docs (`tools/docs/INIT-CLUSTER.md` plus a top-level `README.md`) explain the new layout, the `cd clusters/<name> && nix develop` workflow, how to bump Talos per cluster, and how to add a new cluster with `just new-cluster`.
features.notes: Approved design captured in chat (split tools/ and clusters/ in a monorepo). Migration must preserve the live cluster: tofu state addresses move from root resources to module.cluster.*, verified by tofu plan == no-change. Per-cluster nixpkgs pin is the single source of truth for talosctl + TALOS_VERSION.

features.description: Install cert-manager via Helm and configure a CA-backed ClusterIssuer named after CLUSTER_ISSUER_NAME (default atricore-ca), using the operator-supplied CA materials in clusters/<name>/secrets/.
acceptance: `tools/cluster/cert-manager/{install.sh,uninstall.sh,smoke.sh,helm-values.yaml,cluster-issuer.yaml.tpl,chart-version.txt}` all exist; the `*.sh` files are executable and start with the `${TOOLS_DIR:?...}` preamble.

`tools/.env.example` declares `CLUSTER_ISSUER_NAME=atricore-ca` and `CERT_MANAGER_CHART_VERSION=<pinned>` (matching `chart-version.txt`). The bootstrap.config-scheme test target's required-vars list is extended with both keys.
`tools/Justfile` exposes three `[no-cd]` recipes: `cert-manager-install`, `cert-manager-smoke`, `cert-manager-uninstall`. Install and smoke depend on `env-check`.

`install.sh` pre-validates that `${CA_CERT_PATH}` and `${CA_KEY_PATH}` exist and that their public keys (modulus or pubkey hash) match; warns when the cert's notAfter is less than 60 days away; helm-installs the pinned chart in namespace `cert-manager` with `--create-namespace`; applies the TLS Secret named `${CLUSTER_ISSUER_NAME}` server-side; applies the rendered ClusterIssuer; then runs `cmctl check api --wait=2m`.

`cluster-issuer.yaml.tpl` declares `kind: ClusterIssuer`, `metadata.name: ${CLUSTER_ISSUER_NAME}`, and `spec.ca.secretName: ${CLUSTER_ISSUER_NAME}` so envsubst produces a valid manifest at install time.

Re-running `just cert-manager-install` against an already-installed cluster is a no-op: `helm upgrade --install` keeps the release at the same revision/chart version, and `kubectl apply --server-side` reports unchanged for both Secret and ClusterIssuer.
`just cert-manager-uninstall` removes the ClusterIssuer, the Secret, the Helm release, and the `cert-manager` namespace, and is safe to run when nothing is installed (no errors on missing resources).

`tools/docs/INIT-CLUSTER.md` Phase 2 section names the issuer `atricore-ca` (replacing any prior `own-ca` reference) and documents the three recipes plus the prerequisites (working cluster, kubeconfig, ca.crt + ca.key in cluster's secrets/).

`tools/Makefile` has a new `test-cluster.cert-manager` target wired into the top-level `test` target. It validates offline: file presence and shebangs/preambles; install.sh references helm/kubectl/envsubst/cmctl with the right patterns and includes the openssl pre-validate and 60-day expiry warning; cluster-issuer.yaml.tpl has the required placeholder and shape; chart-version.txt matches `^v[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+$`; the new env vars are in `.env.example`; the new Justfile recipes are present.
`clusters/k8s4/.env` is updated with `CLUSTER_ISSUER_NAME=atricore-ca` and `CERT_MANAGER_CHART_VERSION=<same default>` so `just env-check` continues to pass for the k8s4 cluster.
`README.md` (top-level) references `just cert-manager-install` as the Phase 2 step for adding cert-manager to a cluster; clarifies it is NOT part of `cluster-up`.

All existing structural tests still pass when run as `npm test` from `clusters/k8s4/` (which runs the tools structural tests + the cluster smoke). The new `test-cluster.cert-manager` is part of that run.
features.notes: User-supplied CA at clusters/k8s4/secrets/{ca.crt,ca.key}. CN: atricore-ca. Cert valid until 2026-07-03. Plan-mode tradeoffs were locked in: parameterized issuer name (default atricore-ca), per-cluster cert-manager version pin, no chain-verification in smoke, openssl pre-validation + 60-day expiry warning. cluster-up stays Phase-1; cert-manager is opt-in.

features.description: Install MetalLB in L2 mode via Helm and configure a default IPAddressPool from ${METALLB_RANGE} plus a matching L2Advertisement so Service type=LoadBalancer gets a routable IP.
acceptance: `tools/cluster/metallb/{install.sh,uninstall.sh,smoke.sh,helm-values.yaml,ip-address-pool.yaml.tpl,l2-advertisement.yaml.tpl,chart-version.txt}` all exist; the `*.sh` files are executable and start with the `${TOOLS_DIR:?...}` preamble.

`tools/.env.example` declares `METALLB_CHART_VERSION=<pinned>` (no `v` prefix; matches `chart-version.txt`). The `bootstrap.config-scheme` test target's required-vars list is extended with `METALLB_CHART_VERSION`. The existing `METALLB_RANGE` entry stays as-is.
`tools/Justfile` exposes three `[no-cd]` recipes: `metallb-install`, `metallb-smoke`, `metallb-uninstall`. Install and smoke depend on `env-check`.

`install.sh` pre-validates that `METALLB_RANGE` is a valid CIDR and that neither `${CP_IP}` nor `${WK0_IP}` falls inside it (fail-fast with a clear error); helm-installs the pinned chart in namespace `metallb-system` with `--create-namespace` and `--wait`; applies the rendered IPAddressPool (`default-pool`) and L2Advertisement (`default-l2-adv`) server-side; waits for the IPAddressPool `.status` and the speaker DaemonSet to be Ready.

`ip-address-pool.yaml.tpl` declares `kind: IPAddressPool`, `metadata.name: default-pool`, `metadata.namespace: metallb-system`, and `spec.addresses: ["${METALLB_RANGE}"]` so envsubst produces a valid manifest at install time. `l2-advertisement.yaml.tpl` declares `kind: L2Advertisement`, `metadata.name: default-l2-adv`, `metadata.namespace: metallb-system`, and `spec.ipAddressPools: [default-pool]`.

Re-running `just metallb-install` against an already-installed cluster is a no-op: `helm upgrade --install` keeps the release at the same revision/chart version, and `kubectl apply --server-side` reports unchanged for both the IPAddressPool and the L2Advertisement.

`just metallb-uninstall` removes the L2Advertisement, the IPAddressPool, the Helm release, and the `metallb-system` namespace, and is safe to run when nothing is installed (no errors on missing resources).

`just metallb-smoke` creates a `Service` named `mlb-smoke-test` of type `LoadBalancer` in the `default` namespace (no selector / no backend pods needed), waits for `.status.loadBalancer.ingress[0].ip` to be set within 30s, asserts that IP is inside `${METALLB_RANGE}`, then deletes the Service.

`tools/docs/INIT-CLUSTER.md` Phase 2 section names MetalLB as the first opt-in service and documents `just metallb-install`, `just metallb-smoke`, `just metallb-uninstall`. The configuration table includes `METALLB_CHART_VERSION` alongside the existing `METALLB_RANGE`. The host-route reminder paragraph is preserved.

`tools/Makefile` has a new `test-cluster.metallb` target wired into the top-level `test` target. It validates offline: file presence and shebangs/preambles; install.sh references `helm upgrade --install metallb metallb/metallb`, `kubectl apply --server-side`, `envsubst`, `metallb-system`, the CIDR pre-validate (`METALLB_RANGE` and `CP_IP`/`WK0_IP` cross-check); ip-address-pool.yaml.tpl has the required placeholder `${METALLB_RANGE}` and shape (`kind: IPAddressPool`, `metadata.name: default-pool`); l2-advertisement.yaml.tpl has `kind: L2Advertisement`, `metadata.name: default-l2-adv`, references `default-pool`; chart-version.txt matches `^[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+$`; the new env var is in `.env.example`; the new Justfile recipes are present.
`clusters/k8s4/.env` is updated with `METALLB_CHART_VERSION=<same default>` so `just env-check` continues to pass for the k8s4 cluster.
`README.md` (top-level) references `just metallb-install` as the first Phase 2 step; clarifies it is NOT part of `cluster-up` and points out the host-route reminder.
All existing structural tests still pass when run as `npm test` from `clusters/k8s4/` (which runs the tools structural tests + the cluster smoke). The new `test-cluster.metallb` is part of that run.
features.notes: L2 mode (ARP/NDP), single broadcast domain. Pool/advertisement names hardcoded (default-pool, default-l2-adv). Smoke creates a no-backend Service type=LoadBalancer and verifies an IP from METALLB_RANGE within 30s. cluster-up stays Phase-1; MetalLB is opt-in like cert-manager. Pre-install CIDR sanity check fails fast if CP_IP/WK0_IP overlap METALLB_RANGE. Pinned chart: 0.15.3 (no v prefix; helm's chart version semantics differ from cert-manager).

features.description: Deploy fosrl/newt as a Pangolin tunnel client (userspace WireGuard, egress-only) with credentials from .env, pinned image, restricted PSA, and Recreate update strategy.
acceptance: `tools/cluster/newt/{install.sh,uninstall.sh,smoke.sh,namespace.yaml,secret.yaml.tpl,deployment.yaml.tpl,image-tag.txt}` all exist; `*.sh` are executable and start with the `${TOOLS_DIR:?...}` preamble.

`tools/.env.example` declares `PANGOLIN_ENDPOINT`, `NEWT_ID`, `NEWT_SECRET`, and `NEWT_IMAGE_TAG=<pinned>` (matches `image-tag.txt`). The bootstrap.config-scheme test required-vars list is extended with all four.
`tools/Justfile` exposes three `[no-cd]` recipes: `newt-install`, `newt-smoke`, `newt-uninstall`. Install and smoke depend on `env-check`.

`install.sh` validates that `PANGOLIN_ENDPOINT` looks like an http(s) URL; applies the namespace, then envsubsts and applies the Secret and Deployment server-side; finishes with `kubectl rollout status deployment/newt -n newt --timeout=2m`.
`secret.yaml.tpl` declares `kind: Secret`, `metadata.name: newt-credentials`, `metadata.namespace: newt`, with `stringData` keys `PANGOLIN_ENDPOINT`, `NEWT_ID`, `NEWT_SECRET` rendered from env.

`deployment.yaml.tpl` declares `kind: Deployment`, `metadata.name: newt`, `metadata.namespace: newt`, `spec.strategy.type: Recreate`, container image `fosrl/newt:${NEWT_IMAGE_TAG}`, `envFrom` referencing the `newt-credentials` Secret, securityContext with `runAsNonRoot: true`, `readOnlyRootFilesystem: true`, `capabilities.drop: [ALL]`, and exposes container port 2112 (metrics).
`namespace.yaml` declares `kind: Namespace`, `metadata.name: newt`, with `pod-security.kubernetes.io/enforce: restricted` (newt is fully userspace; no privileges needed).
Re-running `just newt-install` is a no-op: `kubectl apply --server-side` reports unchanged for namespace, Secret, and Deployment.
`just newt-uninstall` removes the Deployment, the Secret, the namespace, and is safe to run when nothing is installed (no errors on missing resources).

`just newt-smoke` runs `kubectl rollout status` against the Deployment with a 60s timeout, then polls pod logs for the literal substring `Connecting to endpoint:` for up to 30s; on timeout it prints the last 30 log lines plus diagnostic hints and exits 1.

`tools/Makefile` has a new `test-cluster.newt` target wired into the top-level `test:` aggregator. Offline assertions: file presence + executable + preamble; install.sh references `kubectl apply --server-side`, `envsubst`, `rollout status`, `PANGOLIN_ENDPOINT`, `NEWT_ID`, `NEWT_SECRET`, `NEWT_IMAGE_TAG`; secret.yaml.tpl has the right kind/name/namespace and three placeholders; deployment.yaml.tpl has Recreate strategy, the image template, the secretRef, runAsNonRoot, readOnlyRootFilesystem; namespace.yaml has the restricted PSA label; smoke.sh references rollout status and the literal `Connecting to endpoint:` string; image-tag.txt matches `^[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+$`; .env.example has the four env keys; Justfile has the three recipes each preceded by `[no-cd]`.

`tools/docs/INIT-CLUSTER.md` Phase 2 section gains a 'newt (Pangolin tunnel client)' subsection covering prerequisites (kubeconfig, Pangolin server reachable, NEWT_ID/SECRET in .env), the three recipes, and the security model (restricted PSA, no node privileges, Recreate strategy).
`README.md` (top-level) references `just newt-install` as a Phase 2 step in the per-cluster workflow; clarifies it is independent of MetalLB and cert-manager and is NOT part of `cluster-up`.

`clusters/k8s4/.env` is updated with non-empty values so `env-check` continues to pass: `PANGOLIN_ENDPOINT=https://app.pangolin.net`, `NEWT_ID=REPLACE-ME-FROM-PANGOLIN-UI`, `NEWT_SECRET=REPLACE-ME-FROM-PANGOLIN-UI`, `NEWT_IMAGE_TAG=1.12.3`. The companion file and INIT-CLUSTER.md document that the REPLACE-ME placeholders MUST be replaced with real values from Pangolin's site-creation UI before running `just newt-install`.
All existing structural tests still pass when run as `npm test` from `clusters/k8s4/` (which runs the tools structural tests + the cluster smoke). The new `test-cluster.newt` is part of that run.
features.notes: Pinned newt 1.12.3 (April 2026 release). No Helm chart. Plain manifests via envsubst + kubectl apply --server-side. Userspace WireGuard via wireguard-go netstack — restricted PSA, no privileges. Egress-only (HTTPS+WSS to Pangolin). Recreate strategy avoids overlapping WebSocket connections during rolls. Smoke greps for 'Connecting to endpoint:' which only logs after successful auth + WebSocket + wg/connect message from Pangolin. Credentials stored in .env (NEWT_ID/NEWT_SECRET); install.sh creates a Secret server-side. cluster-up stays Phase-1; newt is opt-in.

features.description: Install ingress-nginx with restricted PSA, pinned LoadBalancer IP from MetalLB, and a wildcard *.${CLUSTER_DOMAIN} default-SSL Certificate signed by atricore-ca via cert-manager.
acceptance: `tools/cluster/ingress-nginx/{install.sh,uninstall.sh,smoke.sh,helm-values.yaml,default-ssl-cert.yaml.tpl,chart-version.txt}` exist; `*.sh` are executable and start with the `${TOOLS_DIR:?...}` preamble.

`tools/.env.example` declares `INGRESS_NGINX_CHART_VERSION=<pinned>` (matches `chart-version.txt`), `INGRESS_LB_IP=10.4.200.10`, `INGRESS_DEFAULT_TLS_SECRET=ingress-default-tls`. The `bootstrap.config-scheme` test required-vars list is extended with all three.
`tools/Justfile` exposes three `[no-cd]` recipes: `ingress-install`, `ingress-smoke`, `ingress-uninstall`. Install and smoke depend on `env-check`.

`install.sh` validates `INGRESS_LB_IP` falls inside `METALLB_RANGE` using bitmask CIDR math (fail-fast); pre-creates the `ingress-nginx` namespace with `pod-security.kubernetes.io/enforce: restricted`; helm-installs the pinned chart with `--set controller.service.loadBalancerIP=$INGRESS_LB_IP` and `--set controller.extraArgs.default-ssl-certificate=ingress-nginx/$INGRESS_DEFAULT_TLS_SECRET --wait`; envsubsts and applies the wildcard Certificate; waits for it Ready ≤60s.

`helm-values.yaml` declares `controller.ingressClassResource.name=nginx`, `default=true`, `controller.allowSnippetAnnotations: false`, `controller.containerSecurityContext` with `runAsNonRoot: true`, `runAsUser: 101`, `readOnlyRootFilesystem: true`, `allowPrivilegeEscalation: false`, `capabilities.drop: [ALL]`, `capabilities.add: [NET_BIND_SERVICE]`, `defaultBackend.enabled: false`, `controller.service.type: LoadBalancer`.

`default-ssl-cert.yaml.tpl` declares `kind: Certificate`, `metadata.namespace: ingress-nginx`, `metadata.name: ${INGRESS_DEFAULT_TLS_SECRET}`, `spec.secretName: ${INGRESS_DEFAULT_TLS_SECRET}`, `spec.dnsNames` covering `*.${CLUSTER_DOMAIN}` and `${CLUSTER_DOMAIN}`, `spec.issuerRef.name: ${CLUSTER_ISSUER_NAME}`, `spec.issuerRef.kind: ClusterIssuer`.
Re-running `just ingress-install` is a no-op: `helm upgrade --install` reports unchanged; `kubectl apply --server-side` reports unchanged for the namespace and the wildcard Certificate.

`just ingress-uninstall` removes the wildcard Certificate, the default-tls Secret, the Helm release, and the `ingress-nginx` namespace; safe to run when nothing is installed (no errors on missing resources).

`just ingress-smoke` runs `helm -n ingress-nginx status ingress-nginx`, `kubectl rollout status deployment/ingress-nginx-controller -n ingress-nginx --timeout=60s`, and asserts that `Service/ingress-nginx-controller.status.loadBalancer.ingress[0].ip` equals `${INGRESS_LB_IP}` (catches the silent failure where MetalLB couldn't allocate the pinned IP).

`tools/Makefile` has a new `test-cluster.ingress-nginx` target wired into the top-level `test:` aggregator. Offline assertions: file presence + executable + preamble; install.sh references `helm upgrade --install ingress-nginx ingress-nginx/ingress-nginx`, `kubectl apply --server-side`, `loadBalancerIP=`, `default-ssl-certificate=`, `INGRESS_LB_IP`, `INGRESS_DEFAULT_TLS_SECRET`, `pod-security.kubernetes.io/enforce: restricted`, `ip_to_int` (CIDR overlap math); helm-values.yaml has `ingressClassResource:` with `name: nginx` and `default: true`, `allowSnippetAnnotations: false`, `runAsNonRoot: true`, `readOnlyRootFilesystem: true`, `NET_BIND_SERVICE`; default-ssl-cert.yaml.tpl has `kind: Certificate`, `${CLUSTER_DOMAIN}` placeholder, `${CLUSTER_ISSUER_NAME}` placeholder; smoke.sh references `helm status`, `rollout status deployment/ingress-nginx-controller`, `loadBalancer.ingress[0].ip`; chart-version.txt matches `^[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+$`; .env.example has `^INGRESS_NGINX_CHART_VERSION=[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+`, `^INGRESS_LB_IP=`, `^INGRESS_DEFAULT_TLS_SECRET=`; Justfile has the three recipes each preceded by `[no-cd]`.

`tools/docs/INIT-CLUSTER.md` Phase 2 gains an 'ingress-nginx' subsection placed AFTER cert-manager (since it depends on cert-manager + MetalLB): prerequisites, the three recipes, DNS guidance (point A records at `INGRESS_LB_IP` plus the existing host-route reminder), and an example Ingress YAML snippet showing the `cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: atricore-ca` annotation flow. Configuration table gains the three new env keys.

`README.md` (top-level) references `just ingress-install` in the per-cluster Phase 2 workflow as the fourth opt-in step (after metallb/cert-manager/newt); clarifies it depends on cert-manager and MetalLB being installed first and is NOT part of `cluster-up`.
`clusters/k8s4/.env` is updated with `INGRESS_NGINX_CHART_VERSION=4.15.1`, `INGRESS_LB_IP=10.4.200.10`, `INGRESS_DEFAULT_TLS_SECRET=ingress-default-tls` so `just env-check` continues to pass.

All existing structural tests still pass when run as `npm test` from `clusters/k8s4/` (which runs the tools structural tests + the cluster smoke). The new `test-cluster.ingress-nginx` is part of that run.
features.notes:
Pinned ingress-nginx chart 4.15.1 (app v1.15.1). Restricted PSA (NET_BIND_SERVICE allowed). LB IP pinned to 10.4.200.10 inside METALLB_RANGE. Wildcard *.${CLUSTER_DOMAIN} default cert via cert-manager + atricore-ca, wired as controller.extraArgs.default-ssl-certificate so unmatched-SNI clients still get a valid TLS handshake. Snippets disabled (chart default; safer). Smoke is minimal (helm status + rollout status + LB IP allocation check) — no E2E request flow. cluster-up stays Phase-1; ingress is opt-in.</notes>
<parameter name="companionFile">cluster.ingress-nginx.md

features.description: Install metrics-server in kube-system with Talos-friendly kubelet args (--kubelet-insecure-tls, InternalIP-first address resolution) and restricted-grade containerSecurityContext, enabling kubectl top and HPA.
acceptance: `tools/cluster/metrics-server/{install.sh,uninstall.sh,smoke.sh,helm-values.yaml,chart-version.txt}` exist; `*.sh` are executable and start with the `${TOOLS_DIR:?...}` preamble.
`tools/.env.example` declares `METRICS_SERVER_CHART_VERSION=<pinned>` (matches `chart-version.txt`). The `bootstrap.config-scheme` test required-vars list is extended with the new key.
`tools/Justfile` exposes three `[no-cd]` recipes: `metrics-install`, `metrics-smoke`, `metrics-uninstall`. Install and smoke depend on `env-check`.

`install.sh` helm-installs the pinned chart (`metrics-server/metrics-server` from `https://kubernetes-sigs.github.io/metrics-server/`) into the `kube-system` namespace with `--wait`, then waits for `apiservice/v1beta1.metrics.k8s.io` to report `Available=True` within 60s; emits a drift warning when `.env` METRICS_SERVER_CHART_VERSION differs from `chart-version.txt`.

`helm-values.yaml` declares `replicas: 1`; `args` containing both `--kubelet-insecure-tls` and `--kubelet-preferred-address-types=InternalIP,Hostname,ExternalIP`; `apiService.create: true`; restricted-grade containerSecurityContext (`runAsNonRoot: true`, `runAsUser: 1000`, `readOnlyRootFilesystem: true`, `allowPrivilegeEscalation: false`, `capabilities.drop: [ALL]`, `seccompProfile.type: RuntimeDefault`); podSecurityContext with `runAsNonRoot: true` and `seccompProfile.type: RuntimeDefault`.
Re-running `just metrics-install` is a no-op: `helm upgrade --install` reports unchanged.
`just metrics-uninstall` removes the helm release and is safe to run when nothing is installed (no errors on missing resources).

`just metrics-smoke` runs `helm -n kube-system status metrics-server` and `kubectl wait --for=condition=Available --timeout=60s apiservice/v1beta1.metrics.k8s.io` (minimal smoke; full `kubectl top` validation is operator-side).

`tools/Makefile` has a new `test-cluster.metrics-server` target wired into the top-level `test:` aggregator. Offline assertions: file presence + executable + preamble; install.sh references `helm upgrade --install metrics-server metrics-server/metrics-server`, `kube-system`, `apiservice/v1beta1.metrics.k8s.io`, `--wait`; helm-values.yaml has `--kubelet-insecure-tls`, `--kubelet-preferred-address-types=InternalIP`, `apiService:` plus `create: true`, `runAsNonRoot: true`, `readOnlyRootFilesystem: true`, `drop:` followed by `- ALL`; smoke.sh references `helm`, `status metrics-server`, `apiservice/v1beta1.metrics.k8s.io`; chart-version.txt matches `^[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+$`; .env.example has `^METRICS_SERVER_CHART_VERSION=[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+`; Justfile has the three recipes each preceded by `[no-cd]`.

`tools/docs/INIT-CLUSTER.md` Phase 2 gains a 'metrics-server' subsection: prerequisites (working cluster only), the three recipes, what it enables (`kubectl top`, HPA), and the `--kubelet-insecure-tls` rationale. Configuration table gains METRICS_SERVER_CHART_VERSION.
`README.md` (top-level) references `just metrics-install` as Phase 2 step 5 in the per-cluster workflow; clarifies it is independent of other Phase 2 services and NOT part of `cluster-up`.
`clusters/k8s4/.env` is updated with `METRICS_SERVER_CHART_VERSION=3.13.0` so `just env-check` continues to pass.

All existing structural tests still pass when run as `npm test` from `clusters/k8s4/` (which runs the tools structural tests + the cluster smoke). The new `test-cluster.metrics-server` is part of that run.
features.notes:
Pinned chart 3.13.0 (app v0.8.0). Lives in kube-system (canonical home for system addons). --kubelet-insecure-tls because Talos rotates kubelet serving certs and signs them with a per-node CA that's tricky to surface inside the pod; trust boundary is the cluster network. --kubelet-preferred-address-types=InternalIP,Hostname,ExternalIP because the cluster has no DNS for short Hostnames (cp, wk0) — without this metrics-server times out trying to dial cp:10250. readOnlyRootFilesystem=true is fine here (chart's emptyDir at /tmp covers --cert-dir). 1 replica matches our 1+1 cluster. Smoke is minimal (APIService Available only); operator runs kubectl top nodes/pods manually for the full proof. cluster-up stays Phase-1; metrics-server is opt-in.</notes>
<parameter name="companionFile">cluster.metrics-server.md
